phase_1.audio: Sound level monitoring shows stabilization from 44.6 dBA to sustained 37.6 dBA. One or more speech segments identified (avg confidence 0.64), suggesting quiet setting with occasional voices. Overall environment is exceptionally quiet, highly conducive to concentration.
phase_1.vitals: Resting heart rate around 61 bpm observed, reflecting measured attention. Rate remained stable.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS signal strength inconsistent at start (4.4m error margin), achieving 2.9m lock after stabilization. Movement sensors indicate rapid transition to stillness; no significant repositioning detected.

phase_2.correlations: The shift toward sustained stillness aligns with GPS signal stabilization. Audio channel demonstrates minimal variance.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Challenging environmental factors identified: 37.6 dB acoustic baseline, postural adjustments, potential physiological stress.

phase_3.recommendations: Current 228s sessions reasonable; consider testing longer 30-min blocks.

reason: Score determined by comprehensive assessment: ambient noise management (37.6dB), user-state stability (settled), physiological data (available), and sustainable duration (228s).
